observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the MA agreed to draw a JEV to record the following entries:
Debit – Trust Liabilities 2-04-01-010 P 8,705,966.00
Credit - Government Equity 4-03-02-030 P 8,698,119.00
Due to Other Funds 2-03-01-010 P 7,847.00
observations.reason:
The documents for the correcting entries were not submitted for validation.
The transaction documents cannot be traced to the CY 2021 General Ledgers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
a) the GSO use RIS in the issuance of inventories for consolidation in the RSMI and submit both documents to the AU at the end of the month for recording; and
b) the AU:
   i) coordinate with the GSO for the conduct of physical count of undistributed goods at the end of the year so that appropriate adjustment be made restoring the carrying value of undistributed inventory in the books;
   ii) classify goods for distribution as inventory under Welfare Goods for Distribution account, and recognize the same as expense only upon issuance to distribution site, as reported in the RSMI and duly supported with RIS; and
   iii) recognize expenses to their appropriate expense account, to avoid reporting of inaccurate information.
observations.reason:
The LGU did not submit proof of compliance with the audit recommendations.
Documents were not submitted for validation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require:
(a) the BAC Chairman to strictly comply with the posting requirements of the GPPB to ensure accountability and transparency of government transactions, and aid in the accurate reporting by the GPPB of information on government procurements; and
(b) the BAC Chairman and MA to strictly comply with the documentary requirements prescribed by the GPPB on procurements of highly needed goods during the state of national emergency, to avoid possible losses of government funds.
observations.reason: No documents were submitted for validation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the MA submit the following documents:
a. Proof that the personnel and workers have physically reported for work at their respective offices or work stations, such as but not limited to:
   • Approved DTRs/Certificate of Service
   • Accomplishment Reports
   • Logbooks
   • Minutes of the Meetings
b. Authority to report for work during ECQ, for the payments totaling P487,750.00; and
c. Duly received payrolls, for the payment to regular employees amounting P203,750.00.
We also recommended that the MA carefully review the work of the bookkeepers/staff, particularly in the recording of the Municipality’s transactions, to avoid reporting of inaccurate information.
observations.reason: Related audit observations are mentioned in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. draw a Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the net understatement of P95,486.04; 
b. include in the Schedule of Status Report of Unliquidated Cash Advances the outstanding cash advances totaling ₱182,352.96, as basis in the issuance of demand letters to accountable officers withissuance of demand letters; and
c. exert more effort to identify the remaining unaccounted cash advances totaling ₱826,577.05, and immediately apply appropriate actions to settle the same.
observations.reason:
The Accounting Office still to include in the schedule the composition of the outstanding cash advances.
There were still some reconciling items and absence of updated BRS. (Nos. 25 and 26)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Accountant to prepare and submit immediately the Bank Reconciliation Statement for each account maintained with the depository banks, as required under Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE requires the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and General Services Officer be directed to comply with the Accounting and Reporting Procedures on Local Road Asset Management System duties and responsibilities provided in Items V to VII of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.
observations.reason:
There were no disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements.
The Subsidiary Ledgers were not submitted for validation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE requires the designated Municipal Engineer to submit the Bidding Documents, Plans/Drawings, Program of Works and As-Built Plans for the subject infrastructure projects and henceforth, ensure that all contracts and disbursement vouchers submitted to the auditors are supported with complete documentation.
observations.reason: No documents were submitted for validation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Economic Enterprise Officer and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the required physical and financial reports for the economic enterprises of the Municipality, for monitoring of the operating performance and viability of the LEEs and to serve as decision-making tool of the agency.
observations.reason: No documents were submitted for validation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE:
a) Require the Municipal Accountant to: (1) Prepare updated bank reconciliation statements for each bank account and effect adjustments on identified reconciling items, including the unrecorded bank credits totaling ₱596,690.00; and (2) Verify the unaccounted/unidentified balance in the Trust Fund amounting ₱1,595,823.63.
observations.reason:
There were still some reconciling items and absence of updated BRS.
Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the LCE:
Ensure that the LDRRM Office operates effectively, for constituents to receive the exact DRRM services and the benefits therefrom, including the appointment of qualified personnel and creation of position of three (3) staff to supervise the DRRM activities.
observations.reason: No documents were submitted for validation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require:
a. the concerned personnel to expedite the processing of land title in the name of the municipality; and
b. periodic monitoring and follow-up of the processing of the land titling
observations.reason: The LGU designated a personnel who will focus on the land titling. Management still has to ask the Assessor for the monthly status report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the reciprocal accounts in the General Fund, Trust Fund, and Special Education Fund books and effect adjustments to correct the account balances in the Financial Statements.
observations.reason: The reconciliation function cannot be delegated as of this time since there are other issues to be addressed such as the recording adjustments of previous years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct the physical count of the local roads and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System. Likewise, enjoin all concerned department heads to submit a lapsing schedule for the computation of depreciation, maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map, keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card, render a Report on Local Road Network and provide disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the LGU.
observations.reason: Management still has to obtain the detailed list and carry out inventory taking and reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the correcting entries on the errors made affecting Cash in Bank accounts; validate and verify all reconciling items in the Bank Reconciliation Statements and make the necessary adjustment in the books to avoid its repetitive inclusion in subsequent BRS, and provide subsidiary ledgers for each bank account.
observations.reason: Related audit observations are mentioned in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor comply with the requirements provided for in the Notice of Suspension issued on the said transaction.
observations.reason: Validation documents were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant submit immediately the lacking Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents as well as the original copies of cancelled checks.
observations.reason: There were still cancelled/stale/spoiled checks that needed validation; Management to provide the missing documents.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking DVs together with the supporting documents.
observations.reason: Lacking DVs were not completely submitted; management claims some were already submitted in prior years but could not show proof.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to submit the missing documents pursuant to Section 13 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.reason: The LGU still has to ask for update from the Assessor’s Office as to the status of LGU properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record the necessary adjustments to come up with correct account balances.
observations.reason: No documents were submitted for validation.

observations.recommendation:
1.16 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to ;
1. Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Draw a JEV for the erroneous and omitted transactions related to CIB – LCCA;
b. Draw a JEV to recognize an intra-agency receivable from other funds and effect the actual fund transfer.
c. Create a task group to prepare the BRS; and
d. Prepare the subsidiary ledgers for each depository account under the Trust Fund.

1.17 The Municipal Accountant (MA) has drawn correcting entries for the errors. Likewise, the MA confirmed that BRS will be part of the focus and currently preparing subsidiary ledgers for each depository accounts under Trust Fund.

2.4 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to properly account the collections and deposits and submit the RCD to the Accounting Unit on a timely manner, in order to properly account the collections in the year the transactions occurred.

3.10 We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
a. Create an Inventory Committee as required by COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Officer to reconcile the variances between the actual counts and recorded PPEs; and
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to review and draw adjusting entries for the discrepancies in the PPE accounts.

3.11 During the exit conference, the MGSO has committed to have close coordination with the Accounting Unit for PPE reconciliation. Moreover, the MA agreed to draw a JEV for the correcting entries.

4.9 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to effect the corrections on erroneous/unposted accounting entries; and
b. Henceforth, exercise due diligence in the posting of accounts and preparation of journal entries.

5.11 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to draw an adjusting entry to set up the Due from LGUs for the transfer of funds to the recipient barangays and remind her to strictly follow the existing accounting standards in IPSASs and COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 01, 2015.

8.8 We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
a. Ensure the immediate implementation of the PPA’s funded from the 20% Development Fund;
b. Require the MPDC to review developmental projects that are feasible for implementation and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready; and
c. Include in the provisions of the MOA the period of submission of the liquidation reports by the concerned recipients and that the non-implementation or non-liquidation of the transferred funds within the allotted timeframe would disqualify the delinquent barangay/s from being a recipient of financial assistance for future projects.

8.9 The MPDC emphasized on its reply to the AOM No. 2022-001(2021) that as of audit report, at least three (3) projects out of eleven (11) unimplemented PPAs as of December 31, 2021 were fully implemented and completed in CY 2022. While the other eight (8) PPAs are still in the on process of implementation.

9.11 We recommended and the LCE agreed to :
a. Instruct the Administrative Officer or HRMO to meticulously calculate the accumulated leave credit balance of every officer and employee; submit to the Audit Team the Leave Cards and Certification from previous employer; and submit supporting documents on the erroneous terminal leave credit grants; and
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to initiate the recovery through sending demand letter to the retirees with excess payments of TLB, if warranted.

9.12 During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant pointed out that she relied on the submitted documents for the Terminal Leave of retirees hence she did not meticulously recalculate the presented supporting documents in relation to the claims. Henceforth, the MA will now revalidate or recalculate the TLB of the retirees. Likewise, the HRMO committed to submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents for the TLB.

Compliance with Tax Laws
Management complied with the existing BIR rules and regulations and remitted to the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) taxes withheld as of December 31, 2021 totaling ₱5,940,719.08.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (MA) draw the following entries:
Cash in Bank – LCCA 1-01-02-010 P 8,485,000.00
Due to National Government Agencies 2-04-01-010 P 8,485,000.00
We further recommended that the MA faithfully perform her duty by recording financial transactions and operations conformably with generally accepted accounting standards, like the IPSAS, and applicable rules and regulations.

We recommended that the GSO immediately submit to the AU the relevant documents on the donations for proper recording in the books.
Henceforth, the GSO is enjoined to always inform the latter of similar transactions, for immediate recording in the books.
We recommended that Management ensure strict adherence with the laws, rules and regulations on the payment of COVID-19 SRA and other similar allowances, to avoid audit disallowances.

We recommended that the MT should immediately conduct an inventory of all unused checks of the Municipality per bank account, for inclusion in the RAAF and strictly monitor subsequent receipts, issuances and balances using the said report, to arrive at a precise information on all accountable forms of the Municipality, and avoid its possible losses.
We recommended the LCE directs the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the supporting detail/schedules and to provide adequate and necessary disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that the LCE requires the BAC and Secretariat to submit justifications on the non-posting of results of procurements, and the identified lacking documents. Henceforth, follow strictly the provisions in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to avoid legal sanctions.
We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to submit the lacking bidding documents.

We recommended that the LCE require the Local School Board strictly follow the time table in the planning and budgeting of SEF, in accordance with RA 7160 and DepED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular 1, s. 2017.

We recommended that the LCE requires the Accountant to prepare and submit immediately the Bank Reconciliation Statement for each account maintained with the depository banks, as required under Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011.
observations.reason: None posting of irregular interval. The function cannot be delegated as of this time since there are other issues to be addressed (recording and bookkeeping errors, Inventory tracking for depreciation purposes)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE requires the designated Municipal Engineer to submit the Bidding Documents, Plans/Drawings, Program of Works and As-Built Plans for the subject infrastructure projects and henceforth, ensure that there is compliance with required submissions and to execute their respective duties and responsibilities provided in Items V to VII of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.
We recommended the LCE directs the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the supporting detail/schedules and to provide adequate and necessary disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that the LCE requires the BAC and its Secretariat to submit justifications on the non-posting of results of procurements, and the identified lacking documents. Henceforth, follow strictly the provisions in the Revised IRR of RA 9184 to avoid legal sanctions.
observations.reason: None posting of Notices to Proceed

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE requires the designated Municipal Engineer to submit the Bidding Documents, Plans/Drawings, Program of Works and As-Built Plans for the subject infrastructure projects and henceforth, ensure that all contracts and disbursement vouchers submitted to the auditors are supported with complete documentation.
observations.reason: None posting of documents. Henceforth, ensure that all contracts and disbursement vouchers submitted to the auditors are supported with complete documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE requires the Municipal Economic Enterprise Officer and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the required physical and financial reports for the economic enterprises of the Municipality, for monitoring of the operating performance and viability of the LEEs and to serve as decision-making tool of the agency.
We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to submit the lacking bidding documents.

We recommended that the accounting and reporting guidelines for the local road network construction and maintenance system as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 be substantially complied, thus, require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of the local roads and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System. Likewise, enjoin all concerned department heads to submit a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation, maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map, keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card, render a Report on Local Road Network and provide disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: Provide the correcting entries on the errors made on various transactions affecting Cash in Bank accounts; Validate and verify all reconciling items in the Bank Reconciliation Statements and make the necessary adjustment in the books to avoid its repetitive inclusion in subsequent BRS; Provide subsidiary ledgers for each bank account.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor comply with the requirements provided for in the Notice of Suspension issued on the said transaction.
observations.reason: Lacking DVs not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant submit immediately the lacking Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents as well as the original copies of cancelled checks.
observations.reason: Lacking DVs not submitted. Management claims that the subject DVs were already submitted in prior years, but failed to show document to prove the same.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking DVs together with the supporting documents.
observations.reason: Lacking DVs not completely submitted.

observations.recommendation: Management to submit the lacking documents pursuant to Section 13 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
The Municipal Mayor should require the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record the necessary adjustments to come up with correct account balances.

requirement to timely distribute the subsidies to the identified eligible
families within the Municipality, and to liquidate the fund to the DSWD, we
also recommend that the Municipal Accountant (MA) draw the following
entries:
Cash in Bank – LCCA 1-01-02-010 P 8,485,000.00
Due to National Government 2-04-01-010 P 8,485,000.00
Agencies
We further recommended that the MA faithfully perform her duty by
recording financial transactions and operations conformably with generally
accepted accounting standards, like the IPSAS, and applicable rules and
regulations.

We recommended that the GSO immediately submit to the AU the relevant
documents on the donations for proper recording in the books. Henceforth,
the GSO is enjoined to always inform the latter of similar transactions, for
immediate recording in the books.

correcting entries:
Trust Liabilities 2-04-01-010 P 8,705,966.00

a) the GSO use RIS in the issuance of inventories for distribution, for
consolidation in the RSMI and submit both documents to the AU at the
end of the month for recording; and
b) the AU:
i) coordinate with the GSO and the DSWD for the conduct of physical
count of undistributed goods at the end of the year so that
