undistributed inventory in the books;
ii) classify goods for distribution as inventory under Welfare Goods for
Distribution account, and recognize the same as expense only upon
issuance to distribution site, as reported in the RSMI and duly
supported with RIS; and
iii) recognize expenses to their appropriate expense account, to avoid reporting
of inaccurate information.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require: (a) the
BAC Chairman to strictly comply with the posting requirements of the
GPPB to ensure accountability and transparency of government
transactions, and aid in the accurate reporting by the GPPB of information
on government procurements; and (b) the BAC Chairman and MA to strictly
comply with the documentary requirements prescribed by the GPPB on
procurements of highly needed goods during the state of national emergency,
to avoid possible losses of government funds.

We recommended that the MA submit the following documents:
a. Proof that the personnel and workers have physically reported for work
at their respective offices or work stations, such as but not limited to:
• Approved DTRs/Certificate of Service
• Accomplishment Reports
• Logbooks
• Minutes of the Meetings
b. Authority to report for work during ECQ, for the payments totaling
P487,750.00; and
c. Duly received payrolls, for the payment to regular employees amounting
P203,750.00.

We also recommended that the MA carefully review the work of the
bookkeepers/staff, particularly in the recording of the Municipality’s
transactions, to avoid reporting of inaccurate information.

We recommended that Management ensure strict adherence with the laws,
rules and regulations on the payment of COVID-19 SRA and other similar
allowances, to avoid audit disallowances.

We recommend that the MT should immediately conduct an inventory of all
unused checks of the Municipality per bank account, for inclusion in the
RAAF and strictly monitor its subsequent receipts, issuances and balances
using the said report, to arrive at a precise information on all accountable
forms of the Municipality, and avoid its possible losses.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provides depreciation on the LGU’s depreciable assets for fair presentation of the related Expense, and accounts in the Financial Statements.

observations.reason: The Accounting Office still to include in the schedule the Advances for payroll and schedule of outstanding SDO, and still to provide the composition of the outstanding cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE directs the Accountant to draw journal entry voucher to take up the bookkeeping errors so as to bring the Cash Local Treasury account to its desired balance of P14,983.59 and increase the balances of Due to Other Fund and Due to LGU accounts by P39,254.64 and P44,161.38, respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office properly collaborate/communicate with the LCE to ensure that there is sufficient equipage to address the DRRM Operations of the LGU as prescribed in NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1.

We recommended that the LCE requires the Municipal Treasurer to intensify the collection efforts, issue/post Notices of Delinquency, and avail of the remedies for the collection of taxes on Real Properties in accordance with Sections 254, 256, 269 and 470 (d) (2) of Republic Act (RA) No. 7160.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE requires the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the correct amount of taxes from all honoraria payments, and remit the same to the Bureau of Internal Revenue within the reglementary period, pursuant to Sections 5 and 7 of Revenue Regulations No. 11-2018.

We recommended that the LCE requires:

a. The Municipal Accountant to verify the balance of the Due to GSIS account and prepare necessary journal entries to adjust year-end balances;

b. The Municipal Treasurer to remit the withheld funds on time; and

c. All concerned officials to strictly adhere to the provisions cited in PD No. 1445, COA Circular No. 92-382, and IRR of RA No. 8291 on the remittance of GSIS premiums and loan amortizations withheld.

We recommended that the LCE:

a) Require the Municipal Accountant to:

(1) Prepare updated bank reconciliation statements for each bank account and effect adjustments on identified reconciling items, if necessary, including the unrecorded bank credits totaling P596,690.00; and

(2) Verify the unaccounted/unidentified balance in the Trust Fund amounting P1,595,823.63.

(3) Require the Municipal Treasurer to verify the nature and purpose of the funds received totaling P596,690.00 and ensure that the same shall not be utilized while verification is ongoing, to avoid the misapplication of the said fund.

We recommended that the LCE require that reconciliation between accounting and Quedancor bookkeeper be made before Financial Reports are finalized. Moreover, exert more effort to exhaust other available remedies to collect the delinquent accounts, to avoid the loss of government funds.

We recommended to the LCE the following:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly impose the five percent (5%) surcharge on late payment of dues; and

Effect the disconnection of services to the consumers for non-payment of bills for three (3) consecutive months, to avoid the unregulated use of the LGU’s natural water resources.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and Technical Working Group (TWG) to submit the lacking contract documents and henceforth, adhere to the bidding process prescribed under RA No. 9184 and its IRR, including the proper dissemination of bid opportunities and its result, the conduct of a strict evaluation of bids until the post qualification activity, and the submission of complete contract documents.
We recommended that the LCE requires the Municipal Accountant to ensure that only claims with complete supporting documents are processed and for payment.

We recommended that the LCE:

Require the Municipal Budget Officer to maintain the registries for LDRRMF in the Special Trust Fund for more accurate monitoring of the fund; and

Ensure that the LDRRM Office operates effectively, for the constituents to receive the exact DRRM services and the benefits therefrom, including the appointment of qualified personnel and creation of position of three (3) staff to supervise the DRRM activities.

We recommended that the LCE require:

a. the concerned personnel to expedite the processing of land title in the name of the municipality; and

b. all barangays to create BSWM Committee and prepare BSWMP.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (a) provide SL for each of the two (2) bank accounts in the General Fund; prepare and update the BRS for each bank account; verify all reconciling items thereon and effect adjustments, if necessary, including the stale checks; and validate and verify the material unaccounted/ unidentified balance in Trust Fund amounting P2,670,626.19.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the reciprocal accounts in the General Fund, Trust Fund, and Special Education Fund books and effect adjustments to correct the account balances in the Financial Statements. Henceforth, follow the proper accounting procedures in recording and reporting intra-agency transactions, to attain fair presentation in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Local School Board prepare a three-year program for the proper allocation of SEF to schools, and strictly follow the time table in the planning and budgeting of SEF, in accordance with RA 7160 and DepED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular 1, s. 2017.

We recommended that the LCE require the LDRRMO-Designate to ensure that programs, projects and activities included in the LDRRMFIP are implemented in order to enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities. Also, require the Accountant to prepare Monthly Report of LDRRMF Sources and Utilization and Subsidiary Ledgers for LDRRMF transferred to the Trust Fund by year of transfer, and henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 for proper accounting and reporting of LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that contracts and all documents forming part thereof are submitted within the five (5) days from execution pursuant to pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, RA 9184 and PD 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to provide an allowance for depreciation on all of the municipality’s depreciable assets as mandated under existing regulation in order to fairly present the PPE accounts.

We recommended that the municipality maximize utilization of appropriated fund in implementing the various programs for risk reduction management in order to enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities. Likewise consider the creation of the mandatory positions of the Office of the LDRRMO to ensure that programs and projects will be judiciously implemented under their supervision.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to the Inventory Committee to complete, within a specified timeframe, the conduct of the physical count of all properties of LGU Pinabacdao and to prepare the necessary Report on the Physical Count of Property Plant and Equipment within one month from the completion of the inventory-taking. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the RPCPPE with accounting records.

Require the Municipal Mayor submit to the Audit Team, within ten (10) days after the award of each infrastructure project or before the start of program/activity, that appropriate signboards and public notices were already posted, supported with pictures and description of the signboard location.
Management to strictly observe the guidelines on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances and strictly adhere regulations on the proper accounts to be used in recording cash advances.

We advised that Municipal Accountant- Designate who certifies on the vouchers as to the legality and propriety of the claims, as well as the completeness of the supporting documents should affix her signature and process the disbursements only when supported with the required documentation.
It is recommended that every financial transaction should be recorded and correctly recorded in the original book of entry for fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements.
We recommended that the (60) Municipal Mayor comply with the requirements provided for in the Notice of Suspension issued on the said transaction.

We recommended that the (62) Municipal Mayor submit to the Audit Team, within ten (10) days after the award of each infrastructure project or before the start of program/activity, that appropriate signboards and/or public notices were already posted, supported with pictures and description of the signboard location.

1.10 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provides depreciation on the
LGU’s depreciable assets for fair presentation of accounts in the Financial
Statements.

2.12 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the Municipal
Accountant to:
    a. draw a Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the net understatement of
    P95,486.04;
    b. include in the Schedule of Status Report of Unliquidated Cash Advances
    the outstanding cash advances totaling P182,352.96, as basis in the
    issuance of demand letters to accountable officers with unliquidated cash
    advances to date;
    c. recommend the suspension of the salaries of accountable officers who
    continuously fail to submit their liquidation reports;
    d. stop the grant of additional cash advances to employees with previous
    unliquidated cash advances; and
    e. exert more effort to identify the remaining unaccounted cash advances
    totaling P826,577.05, and immediately apply appropriate actions to settle
    the same.

3.11 We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE requires the
Accountant to draw journal entry voucher to take up the bookkeeping errors
so as to bring the Cash Local Treasury account to its desired balance of
P14,983.59 and increase the balances of Due to Other Fund and Due to LGU
accounts by P39,254.64 and P44,161.38, respectively.

4.11 We recommended that the LCE directs the Accountant to prepare and submit
immediately the Bank Reconciliation Statement for each account maintained
with the depository banks, as required under Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and
COA Circular No. 96-011.

5.9 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and
General Services Officer be directed to comply with the Accounting and
Reporting Procedures on Local Road Asset Management System and to
execute their respective duties and responsibilities provided in Items V to VII
of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.

6.6 We recommended the Local Chief Executive directs the Municipal
Accountant to prepare and submit the supporting detail/schedules and to
provide adequate and necessary disclosure in the Notes to Financial
Statements.

7.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the BAC and its
Secretariat to submit justifications on the non-posting of results of
procurements, and the identified lacking documents. Henceforth, follow
strictly the provisions in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to avoid legal
sanctions.

8.9 We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
requires the designated Municipal Engineer to submit the Bidding Documents,
Plans/Drawings, Program of Works, and As-Built Plans for the subject
infrastructure projects, and henceforth, ensure that all contracts and
disbursement vouchers submitted to the auditors are supported with complete
documentation.

9.7 We recommended that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Office properly collaborate/communicate with the LCE to
ensure that there is sufficient equipage to address the DRRM Operations of
the LGU as prescribed in NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2013-1.

10.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the Municipal
Treasurer to intensify the collection efforts, issue/post Notices of Delinquency,
and avail of the remedies for the collection of taxes on Real Properties in
accordance with Sections 254, 256, 269 and 470 (d) (2) of Republic Act (RA)
No. 7160.

11.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the Municipal
Economic Enterprise Officer and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and
submit the required physical and financial reports for the economic
enterprises of the Municipality, for monitoring of the operating performance
and viability of the LEEs and to serve as decision- making tool of the agency.

12.6 We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE requires the
Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the correct
amount of taxes from all honoraria payments, and remit the same to the
Bureau of Internal Revenue within the reglementary period, pursuant to
Sections 5 and 7 of Revenue Regulations No. 11-2018.

13.11 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires:
    a. The Municipal Accountant to verify the balance of the Due to GSIS
    account and prepare necessary journal entries to adjust year-end balances;
    b. The Municipal Treasurer to remit the withheld funds on time;
    c. All concerned officials to strictly adhere to the provisions cited in PD No.
    1445, COA Circular No. 92-382, and IRR of RA No. 8291 on the remittance
    of GSIS premiums and loan amortizations withheld.

14.11 We recommended that the LCE requires:
1. The Municipal Accountant to:
    a. reconcile monthly the amount withheld from payroll/DVs on
    premium contributions against the remittance list;
    b. prepare necessary journal entries to adjust year-end balances and
    submit the lacking Official Receipt;
2. The Municipal Treasurer to remit the withheld funds on time and intact;
3. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to strictly adhere to
    the provisions cited in PD No. 1445, PhilHealth Circular No. 01-2014, and
    IRR of R.A. 7875 on the remittance of premiums and contributions
    withheld. This is without prejudice to the issuance of notice of
    disallowance on the penalties totaling P1, 071.27 which was paid out of
    LGU fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
a) Require the Municipal Accountant to: (1) Prepare updated bank reconciliation statements for each bank account and effect adjustments on identified reconciling items, if necessary, including the unrecorded bank credits totaling P596,690.00; and (2) Verify the unaccounted/unidentified balance in the Trust Fund amounting P1,595,823.63.
(3) Require the Municipal Treasurer to verify the nature and purpose of the funds received totaling P596,690.00 and ensure that the same shall not be utilized while verification is ongoing, to avoid the misapplication of the said fund.

We recommended that the LCE (4) direct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjusting entries to correct the erroneous recording of collections and unrecognized non-cash transactions, and henceforth, (5) require that reconciliation between accounting and Quedancor bookkeeper be made before the Financial Reports are finalized. Moreover, (6) exert more effort to exhaust other available remedies to collect delinquent accounts, to avoid the loss of government funds.

We recommended to the LCE the following: (7) Assess the necessity and number of Job Order personnel assigned in the operation of the PWWS to minimize expenses; (8) Direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books of accounts the unrecorded water bills receivables and include in the special accounts the expenses incurred in the operation of the waterworks system to arrive an accurate information on the results of operations and balances for affected management decision; (9) Require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly impose and collect the five percent (5%) surcharge on late payment of dues; and (10) Effect the disconnection of services to the consumers for non-payment of bills for three (3) consecutive months, to avoid the unregulated use of the LGU’s natural water resources.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (11) ensure that all programs, projects and activities to be funded by the 20% Development Fund are eligible and implemented during the budget year to attain optimum utilization of the fund and full enjoyment of benefits by the populace.

We recommended that the LCE (12) direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and Technical Working Group (TWG) to submit the lacking contract documents and henceforth, (13) adhere to the bidding process prescribed under RA No. 9184 and its IRR, including the proper dissemination of bid opportunities and its result, the conduct of a strict evaluation of bids until the post qualification activity, and the submission of complete contract documents.

We recommended that the LCE (14) direct the Municipal Engineering Office to submit the approved suspension and resumption orders, if any, or an explanation for the delay in project completion, and otherwise collect the corresponding amount of liquidated damages from the contractor. Likewise, (15) require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that only claims with complete supporting documents are processed and for payment.

We recommended that the LCE (16) require the Property Officer to notify the AT of all deliveries, and for him, as well as the Inspection Officer, to fill-out the AIR properly, in order to provide complete information thereon.

We recommended that the (17) BAC and Municipal Accountant make representation with the concerned suppliers and contractors to post warranty securities for the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects that had already been fully paid. Henceforth, (18) strictly observe the mandatory posting of warranty security to guarantee that the contractor shall perform his responsibilities pursuant to Section 62 of the IRR of RA No. 9184 and Section 9.1.1.4 and 9.1.3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the LCE: (19) Require the Municipal Budget Officer to maintain the registries for LDRRMF in the Special Trust Fund for more accurate monitoring of the fund; (20) Require the Municipal Accountant to transfer the amount of P3,064,187.48 to the special trust fund and revise the Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to reflect the correct charging of disbursement; and (21) Ensure that the LDRRM Office operates effectively, for the constituents to receive the exact DRRM services and the benefits therefrom, including the appointment of qualified personnel and creation of position of three (3) staff to supervise the DRRM activities.

We recommended that the LCE (22) require the Accountant to submit the necessary documents to support the recognition of the rice seeds as expense, and reclassify to inventory account any item which remain unissued to date. Likewise, (23) require the BAC to provide the clear description of the unit of issue used on the procured agrivet supplies to determine the exact quantity for delivery by the supplier, and (24) submit the lacking bidding documents.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive require: (25) the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to intensify efforts to have the SWMP approved by the NSWMC; (26) the concerned personnel to expedite the processing of land title in the name of the municipality; and (27) all barangays to create BSWM Committee and prepare BSWMP. We further recommended to (28) intensify efforts to finance the planned procurement of vehicle for garbage collection, and to consider other projects or alternatives for the LGU to operationalize the centralized disposal of its residual wastes.

We recommended that the LCE (29) require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the erroneous entries made on the Due to BIR account and submit the lacking disbursement vouchers (DVs) totaling P63,819.09, as well as the lacking supporting documents on submitted DVs totaling P711,434.18. Also, (30) require all others concerned to strictly comply with the deadlines for the remittance of withheld taxes set forth in BIR Regulations No. 6-2001 and BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular 23-2007.

We recommended that the LCE (31) require the GAD Chairperson to revise the GAD AR and provide adequate disclosure on the attribution of PS to avoid misconception by users of the report, and henceforth, (32) ensure that only expenditures that are addressing/responsive to the selected gender issues are charged to the GAD Fund.

We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to obtain from the supplier a Certificate of Product Registration from the FDA on the drugs and medicines procured, and certification from the MHO that the medicines procured conformed with the PNDF. Likewise, (34) require the Municipal Accountant to submit the necessary documents to support the recognition of inventories as expense, and reclassify to inventory account any item which remain unissued to date.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (35) provide SL for each of the two (2) bank accounts in the General Fund; (36) prepare and update the BRS for each account; (37) verify all reconciling items thereon and effect adjustments, if necessary, including the stale checks; and (38) validate and verify the material unaccounted/ unidentified balance in Trust Fund amounting P2,670,626.19.

We recommended that (39) the Municipal Accountant reconcile the reciprocal accounts in the General Fund, Trust Fund, and Special Education Fund books and effect adjustments to correct the account balances in the Financial Statements. Henceforth, (40) follow the proper accounting procedures in recording and reporting intra-agency transactions, to attain fair presentation in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Local School Board (41) prepare a three-year program for the proper allocation of SEF to schools, and (42) strictly follow the time table in the planning and budgeting of SEF, in accordance with RA 7160 and DepED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular 1, s. 2017.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (43) require the LDRRMO-Designate to ensure that programs, projects and activities included in the LDRRMFIP are implemented in order to enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities. Also, (44) require the Accountant to prepare Monthly Report of LDRRMF Sources and Utilization and Subsidiary Ledgers for LDRRMF transferred to the Trust Fund by year of transfer, and henceforth, (45) strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 for proper accounting and reporting of LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (46) direct the concerned officers and employees to submit the lacking documents; and (47) henceforth, ensure that contracts and all documents forming part thereof are submitted within the five (5) days from execution and (48) all claims are supported with complete documentation before payment, pursuant to pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, RA 9184 and PD 1445.

We recommended that (49) Municipal Accountant be required to provide an allowance for depreciation on all of the municipality’s depreciable assets as mandated under existing regulation in order to fairly present the PPE accounts.

We recommended that the accounting and reporting guidelines for the local road network construction and maintenance system as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 be substantially complied, thus, require (51) the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of the local roads and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System. Likewise, (52) enjoin all concerned department heads to submit a lapsing schedule for the computation of depreciation, maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map, keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card, render a Report on Local Road Network and provide disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the LGU.

We recommended that the (53) municipality maximize utilization of appropriated fund in implementing the various programs for risk reduction management in order to enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities. Likewise (54) consider the creation of the mandatory positions of the Office of the LDRRMO to ensure that programs and projects will be judiciously implemented under their supervision.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (55) Prepare the correcting entries on the errors made on Cash in Bank accounts; (56) Validate and verify all reconciling items in the Bank Reconciliation Statements and make the necessary adjustment in the books to avoid its repetitive inclusion in subsequent BRS, and; (57) Provide subsidiary ledgers for each bank account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (58) issue directive to the Inventory Committee to complete, within a specified timeframe, the conduct of physical count of all properties of LGU Pinabacdao and to prepare the necessary Report on the Physical Count of Property Plant and Equipment within one month from the completion of the inventory-taking. We also recommended that the (59) Municipal Accountant reconcile the RPCPPE with accounting records.

We recommended that (61) the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant submit immediately the lacking Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents as well as the original copies of cancelled checks.
We recommended (63) Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking DVs together with the supporting documents.
observations.reason: (63) Lacking DVs not completely submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended (64) Management submits the lacking documents pursuant to Section 13 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended (49) Management to strictly observe the guidelines on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances and strictly adhere regulations on the proper accounts to be used in recording cash advances.
We recommended (66) The Municipal Mayor should require the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record the necessary adjustments to come up with correct account balances.

We recommended (67) Further, we advised that Municipal Accountant-Designate who certifies on the vouchers as to the legality and propriety of the claims, as well as the completeness of the supporting documents should affix her signature and process the disbursements only when supported with the required documentation.

We recommended (68) It is recommended that every financial transaction be recorded and correctly recorded in the original book of entry for fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements.

1.14 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
a) Require the Municipal Accountant to:
i) Prepare updated bank reconciliation statements for each bank
account and effect adjustments on identified reconciling items, if
necessary, including the unrecorded bank credits totaling
P596,690.00; and
ii) Verify the unaccounted/unidentified balance in the Trust Fund
amounting P1,595,823.63.
b) Require the Municipal Treasurer to verify the nature and purpose of
the funds received totaling P596,690.00 and ensure that the same shall
not be utilized while verification is ongoing, to avoid the misapplication
of the said fund.

2.16 We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to make
necessary adjusting entries to correct the erroneous recording of collections
and unrecognized non-cash transactions, and henceforth, require that
reconciliation between accounting and Quedancor bookkeeper be made
before the Financial Reports are finalized. Moreover, exert more effort to
exhaust other available remedies to collect the delinquent accounts, to avoid
the loss of government funds.

3.13 We recommended to the LCE the following:
1. Assess the necessity and number of Job Order personnel assigned in the
operation of the PWWS to minimize expenses;
2. Direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books of accounts
the unrecorded water bills receivables and include in the special
accounts the expenses incurred in the operation of the waterworks
system to arrive an accurate information on the results of operations
and balances for management decision making-purposes;
3. Require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly impose and collect the five
percent (5%) surcharge on late payment of dues; and
4. Effect the disconnection of services to the consumers for non-payment
of bills for three (3) consecutive months, to avoid the unregulated use of
the LGU’s natural water resources.

4.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that all programs,
projects and activities to be funded by the 20% Development Fund are
eligible and implemented during the budget year to attain optimum utilization
of the fund and full enjoyment of benefits by the populace.

5.12 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC) and Technical Working Group (TWG) to submit the
lacking contract documents and henceforth, adhere to the bidding process
prescribed under RA No. 9184 and its IRR, including the proper
dissemination of bid opportunities and its result, the conduct of a strict
evaluation of bids until the post qualification activity, and the submission of
complete contract documents.

6.9 We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Engineering Office to
submit the approved suspension and resumption orders, if any, or an
explanation for the delay in project completion, otherwise collect the
corresponding amount of liquidated damages from the contractor. Likewise,
require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that only claims with complete
supporting documents are processed and for payment.

7.9 We recommended that the LCE require the Property Officer to notify the AT
of all deliveries, and for him, as well as the Inspection Officer, to fill-out the
AIR properly, in order to provide complete information thereon.

8.9 We recommended that the BAC and Municipal Accountant make
representation with the concerned suppliers and contractors to post warranty
securities for the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects that had
already been fully paid. Henceforth, strictly observe the mandatory posting of
warranty security to guarantee that the contractor shall perform his
responsibilities pursuant to Section 62 of the IRR of RA No. 9184 and Section
9.1.1.4 and 9.1.3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

9.18 We recommended that the LCE:
a) Require the Municipal Budget Officer to maintain the registries for
LDRRMF in the Special Trust Fund for more accurate monitoring of
the fund;
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to transfer the amount of
P3,064,187.48 to the special trust fund and revise the Report on the
Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to reflect the correct charging of
disbursement; and
c) Ensure that the LDRRM Office operates effectively, for the constituents
to receive the exact DRRM services and the benefits therefrom,
including the appointment of qualified personnel and creation of
position of three (3) staff to supervise the DRRM activities.

10.17 We recommended that the LCE require the Accountant to submit the
necessary documents to support the recognition of the rice seeds as expense,
and reclassify to inventory account any item which remain unissued to date.
Likewise, require the BAC to provide the clear description of the unit of issue
used on the procured agrivet supplies to determine the exact quantity for
delivery by the supplier, and submit the lacking bidding documents.

11.5 We recommended that the LCE require:
a) The Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to intensify efforts to
have the SMWP approved by the NSWMC;
b) The concerned personnel to expedite the processing of land title in the
name of the municipality; and
c) All barangays to create BSWM Committee and prepare BSWMP.

11.6 We further recommended to intensify efforts to finance the planned
procurement of vehicle for garbage collection, and to consider other projects
or alternatives for the LGU to operationalize the centralized disposal of its
residual wastes.

12.12 We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to adjust
the erroneous entries made on the Due to BIR account and submit the lacking
disbursement vouchers (DVs) totaling P63,819.09, as well as the lacking
supporting documents on submitted DVs totaling P711,434.18. Also, require
all others concerned to strictly comply with the deadlines for the remittance
of withheld taxes set forth in BIR Regulations No. 6-2001 and BIR Revenue
Memorandum Circular 23-2007.

13.10 We recommended that the LCE require the GAD Chairperson to revise the
GAD AR and provide adequate disclosure on the attribution of PS to avoid
misconception by users of the report, and henceforth, ensure that only
expenditures that are addressing/responsive to the selected gender issues are
charged to the GAD Fund.

14.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC to obtain
from the supplier a Certificate of Product Registration from the FDA on the
drugs and medicines procured, and certification from the MHO that the
medicines procured conformed with the PNDF. Likewise, require the
Municipal Accountant to submit the necessary documents to support the
recognition of inventories as expense, and reclassify to inventory account any
item which remain unissued to date.
We recommend that the municipal mayor require the municipal budget officer to strictly observe the 55% limitation in the annual budget.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 12 AAR CY 2014 Not implemented as of CY 2017.
observations.reason: Budget and actual expenditures for personal services in CY 2017 exceeded the 55% limitation by more than 1 million and 700 thousand, respectively.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit intact all her
collections as well as collections turned over to her by the collectors daily or on
the next banking day, particularly when collections reach P10,00.00 and stop the
practice of encashment of agency checks out of cash collections, pursuant to
Section 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382 and Section 67 (1) & (3) of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (a) provide subsidiary
ledgers for each of the two (2) bank accounts in the General Fund; (b) prepare
and update the BRS for each account; (c) verify all reconciling items thereon
and effect adjustments, if necessary, including the stale checks; and (d) validate
and verify the unaccounted/unidentified balance in Trust Fund amounting
P2,670,626.19.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the reciprocal
accounts in the General Fund, Trust Fund, and Special Education Fund books
and effect adjustments to correct the account balances in the Financial
Statements. Henceforth, follow the proper accounting procedures in recording
and reporting intra-agency transactions, to attain fair presentation in the
Financial Statements.

We recommended that: (i) procurement of FOL products be covered by
an Annual Procurement Plan as required in Section 7.2 of RA 9174; (ii) require
the concerned officers and employees to prepare and submit a Monthly Report
of Official Travel and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption; and (iii)
henceforth, strictly comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 77-61
dated September 26, 1977 in order to control and regulate the use of government
vehicles and consumption of fuel.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Local
Finance Committee to include in the Annual Budget an appropriation equivalent
to 1% of IRA to finance Local Council for the Protection of Children activities
and adopt a comprehensive plan and program for the welfare of the children.

We recommended that the Local School Board prepare a three-year
program for the proper allocation of SEF to schools, and strictly follow the time
table in the planning and budgeting of SEF, in accordance with RA 7160 and
DepED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular 1, s. 2017.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the LDRRMO
to ensure that programs, projects and activities included in the LDRRMFIP are
implemented in order to enhance disaster preparedness and response
capabilities. Also, require the Accountant to prepare Monthly Report of
LDRRMF Sources and Utilization and Subsidiary Ledgers for LDRRMF
transferred to the Trust Fund by year of transfer, and henceforth, strictly
comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12,
2012 for proper accounting and reporting of LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (i) direct the concerned
officers and employees to submit the all the lacking documents; and (ii)
henceforth, ensure that contracts and all documents forming part of thereof are
submitted within the five (5) days from execution and all claims are supported
with complete documentation before payment, pursuant to pertinent provisions
of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, 2012-001 dated June
14, 2012, RA 9184 and PD 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned
officers and employees to: (i) expedite the formulation of internal policy on the
implementation of biometrics machine as the official register to record daily
attendance of its employees; (ii) prepare monthly report of undertime and
absences based on the DTRs to monitor compliance with the CSC policies on
government hours; and (iii) effect the updating of leave cards based thereon, to
avoid the possible loss of government funds in the form of overpayment of leave
benefits.
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to provide an allowance for depreciation on all of the municipality’s depreciable assets in order to present the PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 1 AAR CY 2016 Ordered the GSO to have an inventory of all properties of the LGU, donated or purchased.
observations.reason: Majority of the depreciable assets of the LGU are still not provided with allowance for depreciation. Total depreciation recognized in 2017 was only P246,788.34. (per 2016 AAR, unrecorded allowance for depreciation for 2016 alone amounted to P424,572.46)

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be directed to draw a journal entry voucher to take up the bookkeeping errors so as to bring the account to its desired balance of P394,704.19.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 2 AAR CY 2016 Adjusting entries have been made to correct the errors.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the accounting and reporting guidelines for the local road network be complied with as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 3 AAR CY 2016 The LCE already created Inventory Committee and issued a memo for the conduct of physical count and reconciliation of records of the LGU.
observations.reason: With unidentified items equivalent to 46.69% of the total account balance; Items paid in previous years reflected. Project costs shown were for the on-going projects instead of progress payments. RPCRNS not properly filled-out. No Local Road Property Card.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipality maximize utilization of appropriated fund in implementing the various programs of risk reduction and disaster preparedness and response.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 4 AAR CY 2016 The LCE already created Inventory Committee and required the filling-up of mandatory positions of the Office of the LDRRMO.
observations.reason: The LGU is now monitoring its utilization of the fund as to program and project allocation. The LGU already filled-up the LDRRM Officer position as of CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the strict observance of the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund as provided for in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 5 AAR CY 2016 Development projects included in 2017 budget were all eligible for funding.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Solid Waste Management Board be directed to develop the 10-year Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan that shall promote the long-term management of solid waste as well as integrate the various waste management strategies of the barangays in its jurisdiction. Likewise, intensify the information dissemination and campaign on waste management especially on the segregation and garbage collection.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 6 AAR CY 2016 The LGU required the Barangays for the inclusion of the Material Recovery Facility in their Annual Investment Plan for CY 2018 while the formulation of the 10-year SWMP is ongoing.
observations.reason: 10-Year SWMP not yet finished. Intensify information dissemination and campaign on waste management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the monthly net take home pay of employees will not be reduced to an amount lower than P3,500.00 and observe the prescribed limitations on payroll deductions so that the monthly net take home pay of the employees will not be reduced to an amount lower than P3,500.00.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 7 AAR CY 2016 The LGU is now observing the monthly net take home pay of its employees below P3,500.00 with monthly net take home pay increased from thirty (30) in December 2016 to forty (40) in December 2017.
observations.reason: With lapsing schedule but Inventory showed an unidentified items equivalent to 46.69% of the total account balance; specific employees concerned still within the prescribed net pay.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record and maintain separate books of accounts and maintain Special Accounts in the General Fund for the local water system and Quedancor re-loaned funds.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 8 AAR CY 2016 Prepared separate books of accounts for the Municipality’s Local Water System and the re-loaning of Quedancor Funds under the Management of the LGU; however separate books exclusively for the Quedancor accounts were not submitted.
observations.reason: No books of accounts exclusively for the Quedancor was submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant: a.) Prepare the complete bank reconciliation statements; b.) Review the work of accounting personnel to ensure accurate recording of transactions; c.) Validate and verify all reconciling items in the bank reconciliation statements; d.) make the necessary adjustment in the books to avoid repetitive inclusion in subsequent BRS; and e.) provide subsidiary ledgers for each bank account.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 1 AAR CY 2012 Submitted 11 out of 13 subsidiary ledgers for bank accounts. The Municipal Accountant is exerting efforts to update and prepare the BRS for the remaining accounts.
observations.reason: BRS not complete with recurring and unidentified reconciling items. Ongoing update.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Mayor issue a directive to the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of its properties and prepare the necessary Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment within a specified timeframe and reconcile the RPCPPE with the accounting records.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 2 AAR CY 2015 Conducted the physical count of its properties and submitted the Report on the Physical Count of Property Plant and Equipment; RPCPPE submitted.
observations.reason: Balances of some specific PPE items not reconciled with accounting records. RPCPPE showing a balance of P99,315,480.79, equivalent to 97% of the total cost of PPE as at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the errors using the correct accounts.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 3 AAR CY 2015 Adjusting entries have been made to correct the errors.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record loan payments based on bank documents and update Bank Reconciliation Statements to monitor bank transaction and correct discrepancies between book and bank balances. Also, prepare necessary correcting entries on the accounts affected.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 4 AAR CY 2015 Adjusting entries have been made to correct the errors.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjustment in the books to revert payables which are not supported with valid claimants, or which had been outstanding for two years or more and against which no actual claim has been filed in order to present in the financial statement a credible balance of financial liabilities.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 5 AAR CY 2015 Adjusting entries have been made to correct the errors.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant compute the amount and the Municipal Treasurer prepare the disbursement voucher and transfer the utilized appropriation of the LDRRMF to the depository bank account maintained for LDRRMF. We also recommend that the LDRRMO, the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the report on the sources and utilization of the LDRRMF monthly using the prescribed format in order to provide the Local Chief Executive and the LDRRM Council a regular update on the status of the fund.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 7 AAR CY 2015 Unexpended LDRRMF fund in 2015 and 2014 in 2015 and 2014 have been transferred in 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that payments out of Quedancor Fund are made through issuance of check only and that the bank account of LGU Pinabacdao be provided sufficient quantity of blank checks.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 8 AAR CY 2015 Payments out of Quedancor Fund 2015 have been strictly prohibited.
observations.reason: NS No. 2016-02-100(2015) and 2016-03-100(2015), both dated June 9, 2016, not yet settled.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant submit immediately the lacking Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents as well as the original copies of cancelled checks.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 9 AAR CY 2015 Submitted 27 DVs with total amount of P388,650.18. 6 DVs with total amount of P3,904,939.86 remain unsubmitted as of 2014.
observations.reason: 11 DVs with total amount of P2,495,601.35 per NSSDC No. 2017-002 dated September 20, 2017 and NSSDC No. 2017-003 dated October 23, 2017 not yet submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor submit to the Audit Team, within ten (10) days after the award of each infrastructure project or program, the appropriate signboards and public notices together with pictures and description of the signboard location.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 10 AAR CY 2015 Pictures on posting of appropriate signboards were submitted.
observations.reason: PPAs were not submitted at the beginning of CY 2018, and for the 1st quarter of 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Mayor require the former Accountant to submit the books of accounts and financial reports for CY 2013 to the present Municipal Accountant so that the accuracy of the beginning balances of the CY 2014 financial statements could be ascertained.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 1 AAR CY 2013 Submitted the CY 2013 accounts in August to December 2016 so that the accuracy of the beginning balances ascertained.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to strictly observe the timely submission of accounts and financial reports and require the Accountant to submit the lacking DVs together with the supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 2 AAR CY 2014 Submitted the lacking documents; however 6 DVs with total amount of P3,904,939.86 remain unsubmitted.
observations.reason: 6 DVs with total amount P3,904,939.86 remain unsubmitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the municipal mayor to submit the lacking documents pursuant to Section 13 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 9 AAR CY 2007 The Mayor issued a memo to the assessor’s office for the compliance of required documents.
observations.reason: Supporting documents not yet submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Management to strictly observe the guidelines on the cash advances and time-bound liquidation and strictly adhere to the proper accounts to be used in recording cash advances. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the schedule of Cash Advances and process reimbursement or new cash advance only after liquidation of previous cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 10 AAR CY 2014 The policy “No Processing of Reimbursement or New Cash Advance” was implemented. Demand Letters for overdue accounts were issued.
observations.reason: Schedule of Cash Advances as of previous year-end and Cash Advance balance per GL did not reconcile. Unliquidated outstanding balances outstanding for more than 10 years. Demand letters issued for overdue accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record all transactions in the books of account for fair presentation of the financial statement and require the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record the necessary adjustments to come up with correct account balances.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 3 AAR CY 2012 Submitted Subsidiary Ledgers for identified items; journal entries have been made for prior years' errors as confirmed by the accountant.
observations.reason: Reconciliation ongoing. Subsidiary Ledgers have been maintained for identified items, and journal entries have been made for the prior years' errors as confirmed by the accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary corrections/adjustments in the books of accounts to come up with correct account balances.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 2 AAR CY 2013 The Municipal Accountant was directed to make necessary corrections/adjustments in the accounts to come up with correct balances. Submitted reconciled accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Loans payable account should be reconciled in order to bring the loan payable account and fairly present the same in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 6 AAR CY 2010 Adjusting entries have been made to correct the errors.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all transactions be recorded in the books of account for fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 7 AAR CY 2010 Submitted a certified photocopy of the General Journal entry to record the said IRA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that procurement of goods, services, and infrastructure items be made through public bidding to obtain the price most advantageous to the government. Also designate who certifies the vouchers as to the legality and propriety of the claims, as well as the completeness of the supporting documents and affix her signature on the vouchers.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 8 AAR CY 2009 Procurement through public bidding for infrastructure projects in CY 2009 have been complied. Submission of pertinent documents for CY 2009 being observed.
observations.reason: Submission of complete documents for procurements in CY 2009 through public bidding not completely complied as reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that every financial transaction should be recorded and correctly recorded in the original book of entry for fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 4 AAR CY 2009 Recorded the 2.5M heavy equipment and capitalization of construction and major repair of buildings and other structures not yet made.
observations.reason: Adjustment for the capitalization of the cost of construction and major repair of buildings and other structures not yet made.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that grants and donations from other level of government should be recorded as Income from Grants and Donations.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 7 AAR CY 2009 That grants already recorded by the Accountant as Income from Grants and Donations. Transfers from NGAs were recorded as “Due to Other NGAs”.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that every financial transaction should be recorded in the original book of entry and the General Ledger for fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 8 AAR CY 2009 The advances made to contractor amounting to P2,950,608.00 were already recouped. The advances appearing in the financial statements are current accounts for FY 2015-2017.

observations.recommendation: Management should create a committee to evaluate and monitor the efficiency of the implementation of the livelihood loan projects and review the provisions of the loan agreement to ensure repayment of loans granted.
observations.managementAction: Finding No. 4 AAR CY 2008 Created a committee headed by the Budget Officer to handle the transactions.

observations.reason: Absence of previous years’ records. The LGU committed to make effort to reconcile Cash in Bank base on existing accounting records.
Management explained their predicament on how to comply because of absence of other important prior years records.
The LGU stated they have no idea on how to solve this error.
Reiterated in Part II of this Report
The newly elected Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Accountant explained they cannot yet implement the recommendation because of absence of records on file for quite a long time now.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to provide an
allowance for depreciation on all of the municipality’s depreciable assets as
mandated under existing regulation in order to fairly present the PPE accounts.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be directed to draw a journal
entry voucher to take up the bookkeeping errors so as to the bring the account to its
desired balance of P394,704.19.

We recommend that the accounting and reporting guidelines for the local road
network construction and maintenance system as prescribed in COA Circular No.
2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 be substantially complied, thus, require the
Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of the local roads and submit
the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System. Likewise, enjoin
all concerned department heads to submit a lapsing schedule for the computation
of depreciation, maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map, keep a
complete Local Road Network Property Card, render a Report on Local Road
Network and provide disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the
LGU.

We recommend that the municipality maximize utilization of appropriated
fund in implementing the various programs for risk reduction management in order
to enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities. Likewise consider the
creation of the mandatory positions of the Office of the LDRRMO to ensure that
programs and projects will be judiciously implemented under their supervision.

We recommend for the strict observance of the guidelines on the
appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund as provided for in DILG-DBM
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

We recommend that the Solid Waste Management Board be directed to
develop the 10-year Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan that shall promote the
long-term management of solid waste as well as integrate the various waste
management strategies of the barangays in its jurisdiction. Likewise, intensify
information dissemination and campaign on waste management specially on the
segregation and garbage collection be conducted so that the Municipality’s
constituents will be fully aware of the benefits derived from the program.

We recommend that the officer in charge of payroll preparation be advised
to strictly observe the limitations on payroll deductions so that the monthly net take
home pay of employees will be not be reduced to an amount lower than P3,500.00
pursuant to Section 52 of the General Provisions of RA 10717.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to set-up and
maintain Special Accounts in the General Fund for the Municipality’s Local Water
System and the re-loaning of Quedan Corporation Funds under the Management of
the LGU .

observations.reason: Absence of previous years’ records.
Although not fully liquidated, the percentage of reduction of the Outstanding Balance in CY 2016 were as follows: 2011 & PY- 49.90% 2012 - 0% 2013 - 100% 2014 – 48.11% 2015 – 88.57% 2016 – 70.19%
Only selected properties as of December 31, 2012 were confirmed and included in the RCPPPE and management has yet to submit CY 2016 Inventory Report of PPEs.
The audit team observed that management may conduct biddings on infra projects in CY 2017.
